name: Norwood
heroLine: Stop losing jobs to missed calls on The Parade.
intro: Norwood's Parade is busy - plumbers, electricians, cafe owners compete. AI ensures you never miss a booking.
whyNow: The Parade foot traffic is back to pre-pandemic levels, and competition has never been fiercer. Businesses using automated booking and follow-up are capturing 30% more local customers while others are still playing phone tag.
painPoints: A plumber in Norwood misses 3-4 calls a day on the job — each one a potential $800 booking gone to a competitor
Busy cafe owners on The Parade lose takeaway orders during peak rush because they can't answer the phone
Physio practices get no-shows costing $120 per slot because reminder calls aren't happening consistently

name: Glenelg
heroLine: Turn summer rush into year-round revenue, not chaos.
intro: Glenelg lives and breathes tourism — Jetty Road restaurants, beachfront hotels, and tour operators see wild swings between summer chaos and winter quiet. Staff get overwhelmed during peak season, bookings slip through the cracks, and customer data vanishes into the off-season void. AI systems keep your pipeline full year-round, automatically nurturing past guests and filling slow periods.
whyNow: South Australia's tourism rebound is real, but seasonal businesses that don't capture and reuse customer data are leaving money on the table. Automation turns one-time beach visitors into repeat customers who book direct, not through commission-heavy platforms.
painPoints: Jetty Road restaurants lose 40% of phone bookings during summer rush because hosts can't answer while seating diners
Tour operators watch customers book through competitors just because they responded to an enquiry 2 hours faster
Accommodation providers pay 15-20% commissions to booking platforms when they could've captured direct repeat bookings

name: Unley

intro: King William Road is Unley's professional heart — allied health clinics, lawyers, accountants, and boutique retailers all serve a discerning local crowd. These businesses run on appointments and relationships, but admin overload means follow-ups get forgotten and valuable slots sit empty. AI handles the scheduling grind so you can focus on what actually matters — your clients.
whyNow: Unley's professional services market is crowded, and clients expect instant responses. Practices using automated reminders and self-booking have cut no-shows by half and freed up 10+ hours a week for actual billable work.
painPoints: Allied health practitioners lose $4,000+ monthly to no-shows because manual reminder calls aren't consistent
Professional services waste 2 hours daily playing email tag just to schedule initial consultations
Boutique retailers miss repeat customer opportunities because there's no system to follow up after purchase

name: Prospect

Everyone's stretched thin, juggling quotes, call-backs, and actual work while trying not to miss the next big job. Smart automation handles the admin chaos so you can stay on the tools where you earn money.
whyNow: Prospect's renovation boom means more competition for local trades. Those responding to enquiries within minutes are winning jobs over competitors who take hours — automation makes instant response possible even when you're on a roof.
painPoints: Tradie quoting jobs around Prospect loses 2-3 leads daily because they can't respond to enquiries until 6pm
Prospect Road cafe owners miss catering orders worth $500+ during morning rush when phones go unanswered
Small builders lose track of follow-ups and let $50k renovation jobs slip to competitors with better systems

Financial advisors miss follow-up opportunities with high-value clients because CRM updates get pushed to tomorrow

name: Hindmarsh
heroLine: Stop losing jobs because you're too busy working to answer.
intro: Hindmarsh is trades territory — mechanics, fabricators, industrial contractors, and automotive specialists keeping Adelaide's infrastructure running. When you're under a car or welding onsite, the phone rings unanswered and the job goes to the competitor who picked up. Automated systems capture every enquiry and book work while you keep your hands on the tools.

Fabricators miss urgent commercial repair calls worth $5k+ because they're onsite and can't take the call
